titleStmt.title: Naameh – AD (F.67) – Maslakh Before (2)
WIBARAB Corpus
titleStmt.funder: Funded by the European Research Council under the Grant Agreement 101020127

publicationStmt.distributor: Austrian Center for Digital Humanities and Cultural Heritage

recording.p: Recorded during Lebanon Fieldwork Campaign Ana Iriarte Diez 2023

prefixDef.p: Private URIs using the teiCorpusHeader prefix are pointers to any element in the WIBARAB teiCorpus document.
Private URIs using the share prefix are pointers to audio file residing on the WIBARAB network share.
Private URIs using the geo prefix are pointers to the xml:id attribute on a place element in VICAV Geodata list.
Private URIs using the dmp prefix are pointers to the xml:id attribute on an element in the WIBARAB DMP document..
Private URIs using the zotid prefix are pointers to the xml:id attribute on a biblStruct element in the TEI export of the VICAV Zotero Group library.
Private URIs using the sources prefix are pointers to the xml:id attribute on an element in the WIBARAB list of sources.

spanGrp.span: And after leaving the Maslakh you went to Dekwaneh right?
yes.
Meaning, Tel al-Zaatar.
No, there is (Tel al-) Zaatar, the Palestinian camp and there is Dekwaneh.
We were, our hourse was split, between (Tel) al-Zaatar and Dekwaneh.
Ah. between al-Zaatar and al-Dekwaneh this is what is called al-Zaatar?
Yes. The Palestinian camp is called Tel al-Zaatar. But I prefer the Maslakh.
Maslakh?
Yes, of course.
To be honest I... My father was raised, was born and grew up in al-Maslakh, and we were born and grew up in al-Maslakh. I prefer the Maslakh whatever happens.
I understand you.
and now, right now if someone gives me the choice I tell them no, I go to the Maslakh.
Everything was easy, everyone liked each other. No one held grudges against each other.
You don't look if this person for example is from the Aransa or from the Neem or from, or this one is not... I did not know I was bedouin myself until we left Tel Al-Zaatar.
Alright. And those who went to Tel al-Zaatar they are just from the Kredin right? Or...
Them, the Kredin in the first place.
From the beginning they were there, in Tel al-Zaatar?
There were people in Borgholiyeh, there were people... But we were born and raised in al-Malakh.
yes.When the events happened in Maslakh, people left to Dekwaneh because there were Kredin in there?
No, it was only us.
Ah.
We were the only Kredin and my uncle, may he rest in peace. My uncle had nine children that were killed in the Maslakh (in the Karantina massacre)
They came wanting to rape the girl
Oh my God
yes.
So they shot the whole family
Four girls and their mother and three guys and their father.
do you remember wasn't it before you or...?
no, I was going. we were in al-Dekwaneh.
but those begging and everyone saw them.
by God.
Yes so many people died in the Maslakh (massacre).foreigners and relatives.
I tell you I prefer al-Maslakh to everything else.
Ah, and how many (years) did you stay in Tel... in Tel al-Zaatar then again?
We stayed in Tel al-Z... in al-Dekwaneh approximately nine years, but most of our days we were in al-Maslakh #
My father stayed in Maslakh and my grandmother and my grandfather and my brother A, may God rest his soul. When he married of course he... But us, we spent all our days in Maslakh.
I see.
This to tell you... I wish the Maslakh (days) would come back. Everytime I bring it up, I cry. Really. The best days of our life.
My God
People used to love each other ...
There was love. People were, in times of joy, people were united, and in times of mourning the slaughterhouse would close for seven days when someone died.
would close
Yes. And they would treat foreigners very well.
How many (people) from the Horan settled? All of them , all of them you can see, they do not have grudges.
Not like (what happened) after...
/laughs/
